code.title: NestJS Module Structure
items: Organize code into feature modules; each module owns its controller, service, and repository
Use dependency injection via constructor parameters; avoid manual instantiation
Use DTOs with class-validator decorators for request validation
Use class-transformer for serialization and deserialization

code.title: NestJS Patterns
items: Use Guards for authentication and authorization
Use Interceptors for cross-cutting concerns like logging and response transformation
Use Pipes for input validation and transformation
Use Exception Filters to handle and format errors consistently
Prefer async/await over callbacks and raw Promises

testing.title: NestJS Testing
items: Use NestJS testing utilities (Test.createTestingModule) for unit tests
Mock providers using jest.fn() or custom mock classes
Write e2e tests using supertest against the full NestJS app
Test each layer (controller, service, repository) in isolation
